questions.q: In which year was the legendary Indian playback singer Asha Bhosle born?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle was born on 8 September 1933 in Sangli, in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle is the younger sister of which other famous Indian playback singer?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle is the younger sister of Lata Mangeshkar, both daughters of the singer and stage actor Deenanath Mangeshkar.

questions.q: Which music director, famous for his innovative compositions, did Asha Bhosle later marry?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle married composer Rahul Dev Burman, with whom she had a long and celebrated musical collaboration.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle was the daughter of which classical singer and stage actor?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle was the daughter of Deenanath Mangeshkar, a noted Marathi stage actor and classical singer.

questions.q: In which language film industry, besides Hindi, did Asha Bhosle record a very large number of songs, given her Maharashtrian roots?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle recorded prolifically in Marathi cinema and Marathi music in addition to Hindi film songs.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Asha Bhosle born in 1933?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle was born in Sangli, which lies in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: For her enormous and stylistically varied output, Asha Bhosle is often described by which epithet?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle is renowned as a 'mistress of versatility' for singing across genres from cabaret to ghazal and classical.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's singing career began primarily as a performer in which field?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle made her name as a playback singer for Indian films, beginning in the 1940s.

questions.q: Which genre of seductive, club-style film songs is Asha Bhosle particularly celebrated for popularising?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle became especially associated with glamorous cabaret and club songs in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: In what decade did Asha Bhosle's playback singing career begin?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle began her playback career in the 1940s, while still a teenager.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in which year?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2000 for her contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: With which composer did Asha Bhosle create a famous run of songs in the 1950s and 1960s, including hits from Naya Daur and Howrah Bridge?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle's celebrated 1950s-60s partnership with O. P. Nayyar produced hits in films such as Naya Daur and Howrah Bridge.

questions.q: The popular cabaret song 'Piya Tu Ab To Aaja' from the film Caravan, sung by Asha Bhosle, was composed by which music director?
questions.explanation: 'Piya Tu Ab To Aaja' from Caravan (1971) was composed by R. D. Burman and became one of Asha Bhosle's signature numbers.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's iconic song 'Dum Maro Dum' appeared in which 1971 Dev Anand-produced film?
questions.explanation: 'Dum Maro Dum', composed by R. D. Burman, was sung by Asha Bhosle for the 1971 film Hare Rama Hare Krishna.

questions.q: The 1981 film Umrao Jaan featured acclaimed ghazals sung by Asha Bhosle, set to music by which composer?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle's ghazals in Umrao Jaan (1981), including 'Dil Cheez Kya Hai', were composed by Khayyam and won her a National Film Award.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle won her first National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for songs from which 1981 film?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for Umrao Jaan in 1981.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle won a second National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for the songs of which 1987 R. D. Burman film?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle won her second National Award for the songs of Ijaazat (1987), including 'Mera Kuchh Saamaan'.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's younger brother, also a child of Deenanath Mangeshkar, is which noted music director and singer?
questions.explanation: Hridaynath Mangeshkar, the youngest sibling of Asha Bhosle and Lata Mangeshkar, is a celebrated music director and singer in Marathi and Hindi music.

questions.q: The American string quartet that recorded the 2005 album 'You've Stolen My Heart', featuring Asha Bhosle singing R. D. Burman songs, was named what?
questions.explanation: The Kronos Quartet collaborated with Asha Bhosle on the Grammy-nominated 2005 album 'You've Stolen My Heart'.

questions.q: The British band Cornershop paid tribute to Asha Bhosle in which 1997 hit single?
options: Good to Be on the Road
questions.explanation: Cornershop's 1997 single 'Brimful of Asha' was a tribute to Asha Bhosle and reached number one in the UK in a remixed version.

questions.q: Across her career Asha Bhosle recorded songs in approximately how many languages?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle recorded songs in more than a dozen Indian and foreign languages over her long career.

questions.q: Before marrying R. D. Burman, Asha Bhosle was first married at a young age to a man named what?
questions.explanation: Asha eloped with and married Ganpatrao Bhosle in her teens, taking the surname she kept throughout her career.

questions.q: The duet 'Chura Liya Hai Tumne', sung by Asha Bhosle and Mohammed Rafi, appeared in which 1973 film?
questions.explanation: The R. D. Burman duet 'Chura Liya Hai Tumne', sung by Asha Bhosle and Mohammed Rafi, featured in Yaadon Ki Baaraat (1973).

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's children carried on the family's musical line; what was the profession of her eldest son, Hemant Bhosle?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle's son Hemant Bhosle worked as a music director in the Hindi film industry.

questions.q: The veteran composer S. D. Burman, who gave Asha Bhosle significant early Hindi film assignments, was the father of which music director she later married?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was the father of R. D. Burman, the composer Asha Bhosle eventually married.

questions.q: In 2013, Asha Bhosle made her acting debut in a lead film role, playing the title character in which movie?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle made her acting debut in the 2013 film Mai, playing an elderly mother in the title role.

questions.q: The R. D. Burman film 1942: A Love Story, with songs partly recorded by Asha Bhosle, was released in which year, the year of Burman's death?
questions.explanation: 1942: A Love Story was released in 1994, the year R. D. Burman died, and is regarded as one of his finest final scores.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's vibrant song 'Yeh Mera Dil' from the 1978 film Don was later sampled internationally in which 2005 hit by the Black Eyed Peas?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle's 'Yeh Mera Dil' from Don was sampled in the Black Eyed Peas' 'Don't Phunk with My Heart' (2005).

questions.q: For what achievement was Asha Bhosle recognised by Guinness World Records in 2011?
options: Most albums released in one year
Most studio recordings by a single artist
questions.explanation: In 2011 Guinness World Records recognised Asha Bhosle as the most recorded artist in music history.

questions.q: Asha Bhosle's classic song 'Raat Akeli Hai' featured in which 1967 thriller starring Dev Anand and scored by S. D. Burman?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle sang 'Raat Akeli Hai' for the 1967 film Jewel Thief, composed by S. D. Burman.
